title: Cities incorporate equity in their climate policies but overlook procedural justice in decision-making
data_sources: C40 Knowledge Hub climate action plans

data_scope.primary_corpus: climate action plans of C40 cities (exact N not stated in abstract/data statement)
data_scope.headline_finding: fewer than half of C40 cities with CAPs lean toward procedural justice
data_scope.derived_data: topic-model corpus available on request (not in open repo)

core_conclusion: Across C40 cities with climate action plans, fewer than half lean toward embedding procedural justice (fair, inclusive decision processes), even though distributive equity language is common. Equity-pledge adoption, later C40 membership and Global-South location correlate with stronger procedural-justice integration; cities lacking it emphasize outcome fairness while excluding marginalized groups, producing tokenistic participation. Cities need concrete organizational tools, not normative prescription.
why_it_matters: Names and measures a blind spot in urban climate governance (fair processes vs. fair outcomes); operationalizes 'tokenistic participation'; shows that pledge adoption, later membership and Global-South location predict stronger procedural justice; points to actionable organizational levers.
why_nature: Cross-city comparative evidence over the C40 network rather than single cases; couples social science with computational text analysis; centers justice, inclusion and marginalization in urban decision-making; directly policy-translatable for city climate networks.
added_value_to_my_research: Geolocate procedural-justice labels onto exposure/marginalization maps -> spatial validation via 2SFCA equity layers
Plan-to-practice gap modeling via multi-source fusion + explainable attribution (open-science pipeline)
Embedding-based justice-aware text classifier as a modality for geospatial foundation-model fine-tuning
Explainable ML + MGWR to test non-stationary drivers of procedural-justice integration
Procedural-justice constraints embedded in facility-siting -> efficiency-equity-procedure trade-off extending EV-charging optimization
